sections.title: Company Overview
sections.subtitle: Who we are
sections.body:
Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is a digital transformation and technology partner of choice for industry-leading organizations worldwide. Since its inception in 1968, TCS has upheld the highest standards of innovation, engineering excellence and customer service.

Rooted in the heritage of the Tata Group, TCS is focused on creating long-term value for its clients, its investors, its associates, and the community at large.

With a global presence spanning 55 countries and over 607,000 highly skilled associates, TCS is one of the largest IT services companies in the world.
sections.highlight: TCS crossed the US $30 billion revenue milestone in FY 2025
sections.quote: We are the partner of choice for enterprises building AI-first cultures.

left.heading: Our Vision
left.body: To be the world's most valued and trusted partner in every client engagement, enabling their transformation to AI-first, cloud-native enterprises.

right.heading: Our Mission
right.body: To help customers achieve their business objectives by providing innovative, best-in-class consulting, IT solutions and services, and to make it a joy for all stakeholders to work with us.

sections.title: Theme of the Year
sections.body:
The TCS FY 2024-25 Integrated Annual Report highlights the rapidly changing world and the role of technology in addressing complex business problems. Recent years have seen unabating transformation, driven by emerging technologies, geopolitical dynamics, and shifting consumer expectations.

In this environment, the most resilient organizations are those that have become Perpetually Adaptive Enterprises — companies that sense, respond, and evolve continuously rather than episodically. TCS partners with such enterprises to embed adaptability into their DNA.

From AI-led modernization to cloud-native architectures, from GenAI-powered customer experiences to intelligent supply chains — TCS is enabling clients to build the adaptive capabilities needed for long-term competitive advantage.
sections.highlight: The Perpetually Adaptive Enterprise is one that senses change in real time and reconfigures itself continuously.

sections.text: The single most transformative force in 2024 was Generative AI. GenAI is not just another tech cycle — it is a civilizational shift.

sections.body:
Dear Shareholders,

The financial year 2025 was a year of profound global disruption. Widespread geopolitical conflicts, military escalations, and uncertain trade dynamics severely impacted global supply chains. Over 60 nations went to the polls, stalling policy continuity and reform agendas across several key markets.

In the face of this turbulence, your company demonstrated exceptional resilience. We partnered closely with our clients to help them perform under pressure while accelerating their transformation agendas. By harnessing the power of Generative AI and other emerging technologies, we enabled our clients to prepare for the future.

For example, we worked with a client to develop a GenAI-based drug discovery solution that created over 1,300 molecules for a concept target. For another client, we implemented a direct-to-consumer digital solution for the automotive industry. In another case, we modernized 50 million lines of legacy code into a modern AI-powered architecture.

These efforts contributed to a robust financial performance, with FY 2025 revenue of US $30 billion and an industry-leading operating margin of 24.3%. Your company was once again recognized as a Global Top Employer for the tenth successive year.

The evolving geopolitical landscape and the emergence of a multipolar world are now reshaping how global businesses operate. We are witnessing the rise of new paradigms, demanding a thorough reimagination of systems, processes, and technologies. TCS is proactively leading this change — we have systematically infused AI across our offerings and built intelligent agent solutions throughout the value chain.

Looking ahead, we plan four distinct progressions: establishing AI agents working alongside our human workforce; delivering solutions through a human+AI model; investing in AI data centers and cloud infrastructure; and forging industry-best partnerships.

We remain steadfastly committed to leading the industry with purpose, innovation, and responsibility. Thank you for your continued trust and support.
sections.quote: I believe every industry stands to benefit from GenAI — and the IT industry is no exception.

sections.text: Significant innovation is happening in the AI ecosystem across infrastructure, data platforms, models and AI-native business applications.

sections.body:
Dear Stakeholders,

I am pleased to share that in FY 2025, we achieved two extraordinary milestones — crossing the US $30 billion mark in revenues and the US $20 billion mark in brand value. This success is a testament to the early investments we have made in strengthening client relationships, building intellectual property, and deepening employee capabilities.

In a very challenging geopolitical and macro-economic environment, the company reported an annual revenue of ₹255,324 crore in FY 2025, growing 6.0% year-on-year (4.2% in constant currency). Our disciplined execution delivered industry-leading margins of 24.3%, even as we accelerated investments in talent.

Our net margin was at 19.0%. Earnings per share were at ₹134.19, a growth of 5.1% over the prior year. The Board has recommended a final dividend of ₹30 per share, bringing the total dividend for the year to ₹126 per share.

We reported a robust Total Contract Value (TCV) of US $39.4 billion — a balanced mix of large, medium and small deals. Our customer-centric focus is well recognized: we were ranked #1 for customer satisfaction in Europe for the 12th year in a row.
sections.quote: We achieved two extraordinary milestones — crossing US $30B in revenues and US $20B in brand value.

subsections.text: Our AI Strategy — Six Focus Areas

items: Establishing AI Centers of Excellence for scalable Enterprise AI strategies
Creating AI Labs for technology experimentation with clients
Building safe, secure, scalable GenAI as a Service Platforms
Providing domain AI solutions with over 150 AI agents
Delivering GenAI-infused services for Software Engineering and IT operations
Setting up sovereign secure Cloud with integrated AI

sections.title: Business Environment
sections.body:
FY 2025 was characterized by an unusually complex and uncertain macro-environment. Persistent geopolitical tensions, evolving trade policies, and multi-year high interest rates weighed on discretionary technology spending. At the same time, the rapid advancement of Generative AI created urgency around transformation, as enterprises recognized the need to modernize their technology estates to remain competitive.

Despite these headwinds, TCS demonstrated resilience rooted in its diversified portfolio, deep client relationships, and investment in emerging capabilities. The company maintained industry-leading margins while continuing to invest in talent, AI capabilities, and client-facing platforms.

sections.title: Income Statement Summary
sections.caption: All figures in INR Crore unless stated. EPS in INR per share.

sections.title: Segment-wise Performance
sections.body:
Banking, Financial Services & Insurance (BFSI): Revenue of ₹89,000 crore, contributing 34.9% of total revenue. Growth driven by regulatory technology, cloud migration, and GenAI adoption in retail banking.

Manufacturing: Revenue of ₹36,400 crore, contributing 14.3%. Strong demand for supply chain resilience solutions and smart factory implementations.

Consumer Business Group: Revenue of ₹28,100 crore, contributing 11.0%. E-commerce platform modernization and omni-channel transformation drove growth.

Technology & Services: Revenue of ₹25,800 crore, contributing 10.1%. AI-led modernization and cloud-native development fueled demand.

chapters.title: Strategy & Growth
chapters.subtitle: Business strategy, expansion plans, and innovation initiatives

sections.title: Strategic Priorities
sections.body: TCS's strategy for FY 2025 and beyond is anchored on three pillars: becoming the AI-first transformation partner of choice, building a future-ready workforce at scale, and deepening long-term client relationships through outcome-based engagements.
sections.highlight: TCS will have the largest AI-trained workforce in the IT industry by 2025.

subsections.text: TCS is embedding AI across its entire portfolio — from software engineering and IT operations to business process services. The launch of TCS WisdomNext™ marks a step-change in how the company delivers AI-native solutions to enterprise clients.

TCS is investing in sovereign cloud capabilities, hybrid cloud management, and edge computing infrastructure. These investments position TCS as a one-stop partner for enterprises navigating complex multi-cloud environments.

With over 607,000 associates trained on AI and emerging technologies, TCS's human capital strategy is centered on continuous reskilling, contextual learning, and building an inclusive, purpose-driven culture.

sections.title: Key Partnerships & Collaborations FY 2025
items: Northern Trust — next-generation global securities platform
Primark — technology transformation across 17 countries in Europe and the US
Xerox — full technology consolidation and GenAI integration
Air New Zealand — 5-year digital transformation partnership
DNB Bank — strategic collaboration for cloud-based banking modernization

items.title: TCS Pace™ Port Network
items.description: Expanded co-innovation network to 30 locations globally for rapid prototyping with clients and startups.

items.description: Established dedicated Generative AI practice, training 100,000+ associates in 12 months.

items.title: AI.Cloud Unit
items.description: Formed AI.Cloud business unit combining cloud infrastructure and AI capabilities into a single offering.

items.title: TCS WisdomNext™
items.description: Launched enterprise GenAI platform enabling agentic AI workflows across client enterprises.

left.heading: Environmental
left.body: TCS has committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2030. The company reduced absolute carbon emissions by 70% since FY 2016 through energy efficiency programs, renewable energy procurement, and green building certifications across its campus network. All TCS campuses are ISO 50001 certified.

right.heading: Social
right.body: TCS's Xcelerate scholarship program supported 50,000 students in FY 2025. The BridgeIT program provided digital literacy training to over 1 million youth in rural India. TCS iON delivered 130 million assessments across 16 countries, empowering education at scale.

sections.title: Governance
sections.body:
TCS maintains highest standards of corporate governance supported by an independent Board with 62.5% independent directors. The company has zero tolerance for corruption, maintaining a comprehensive Code of Conduct, whistleblower mechanisms, and regular ethics training across all geographies.

TCS is included in the MSCI ESG Leaders Index, Dow Jones Sustainability Index, and FTSE4Good Index Series.

sections.title: Risk Management Framework
sections.body: TCS employs an Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) framework aligned with ISO 31000 standards. Risks are identified, assessed, and monitored through a three-tier governance structure — Board-level Risk Management Committee, Executive Risk Council, and Business Unit Risk Officers.

sections.title: Key Risk Register
rows: Trade restrictions, sanctions, and political instability affecting global operations
Geographic diversification; local delivery centers in 55 countries
Cybersecurity threats, data breaches, and rapid AI disruption
ISO 27001 certification; 24x7 SOC; continuous AI capability investment
Attrition, skills gap in emerging technologies like GenAI
Slowdown in IT spending due to macro uncertainty
Diversified client portfolio; long-term contracts; strong TCV pipeline
Data privacy laws (GDPR, DPDP Act) and cross-border data restrictions
Dedicated compliance teams; sovereign cloud offerings; legal monitoring
High revenue dependency on BFSI and North America segments
Increasing mix in Manufacturing, Retail, and Growth Markets
sections.caption: Risk ratings as assessed by the Risk Management Committee, FY 2025

sections.title: Board Statement
sections.body:
Dear Members,

The Board of Directors of Tata Consultancy Services Limited is pleased to present the Integrated Annual Report along with the audited financial statements for the financial year ended March 31, 2025.

The company achieved revenue of ₹2,55,324 crore in FY 2025, growing 6.0% over FY 2024. Net income for the year was ₹48,553 crore. The Board recommends a final dividend of ₹30 per share (face value ₹1 per share) for FY 2025, bringing the total dividend for the year to ₹126 per share.

The company has complied with all applicable provisions of the Companies Act 2013, SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements Regulations 2015, and other applicable laws across all jurisdictions.

sections.title: Key Statutory Disclosures
items: No material changes or commitments affecting the financial position occurred between March 31, 2025 and the date of this report
The company has not accepted any deposits under Chapter V of the Companies Act, 2013
There are no significant or material orders passed by regulators, courts, or tribunals
The company has complied with Secretarial Standards SS-1 and SS-2 issued by ICSI
No fraud has been reported by the Auditors under Section 143(12) of the Companies Act
The company has adequate internal financial controls and they are operating effectively
